etymology.description: 𠁿 -> something sweet in an open mouth -> 甘
etymology.interpretation: 甘 (gān, "sweet") is an ideogram depicting something sweet inside an open mouth. This visualization of taste illustrates the core concept of sweetness as directly related to the act of consuming something pleasant. This visual representation connects the sensory experience of sweetness with its meaning in the character.

examples.chinese: 他的话如甘露滋润了我的心(他的話如甘露滋潤了我的心)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de huà rú gānlù zīrùnle wǒ de xīn.
examples.english: His words nourished my heart like sweet dew.

examples.chinese: 这种水果味道甘美(這種水果味道甘美)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhǒng shuǐguǒ wèidào gān měi.
examples.english: The taste of this fruit is sweet and delicious.
